rationale: The citing paper explicitly describes Luo, Kannan, and Ratchford (2008) as modeling subjective perceptions that vary with task attributes, and then states that this stream provides methodological foundations for integrating conjoint/choice data with attitudinal latent variables. It uses that prior work to position its own contribution as addressing a gap: prior models did not tackle brand-image associations and associated halo/general-factor issues. This is more than a passing mention because the cited paper helps define the methodological baseline the new model is built from and differentiated against.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Luo et al.'s latent-variable integration of attitudinal perceptions with choice/conjoint modeling, then extends that line to brand-image associations by introducing a higher-order factor structure that separates general and specific brand-image effects.
